notes.anchor: the senatus consultum which has been passed about me
notes.note: A senatus consultum was a formal resolution of the Senate; this one, of 1 January 57 BC, commended Cicero's recall to the people and opened the legislative campaign that would bring him home.
A senatorial decree could only recommend; Cicero's recall still required a law (lex) voted by the people, which is the legislation he resolves to await.

notes.anchor: I shall use the authority of the Senate
notes.note: If the bill were blocked, Cicero means to invoke the Senate's auctoritas --- its moral weight rather than binding force --- as warrant for returning even at the risk of his life.
